name: KrillApp.Server.SerialDevice
title: Serial Device
description: Communicates with hardware devices connected via serial ports (USB, UART). Supports read and write operations with configurable baud rate, data bits, parity, stop bits, flow control, encoding, and line terminator. Can send commands to devices and receive data. Received data is stored in the device node's own meta.snapshot; nodes observing the device are invoked on new data. Integrates with Zigbee networks, Adafruit QTPY, Atlas Scientific, and other serial-based sensors and microcontrollers.
shortDescription: Communicates with hardware devices via serial ports with configurable protocol settings.
llmPurpose: Reads from and writes to hardware devices connected via serial ports (USB, UART) with configurable communication protocol settings.
llmBehavior: configurable baud rate, data bits, parity, stop bits, flow control
stores received data in its own meta.snapshot; observers are invoked on new data
is a data-source type — DataPoints persist its readings by observing it (sources+inputs)
llmInputs: send command string for read operations
llmOutputs: serial data stored in own meta.snapshot; observers pull it
llmTypicalUseCases: read sensor data from USB device
llmCreationHints: Create a SerialDevice when the user has hardware connected via USB or UART serial port. Configure the port path, baud rate, and operation mode. Typically auto-detected by the server. To persist its readings, wire a DataPoint to observe the device (the DataPoint lists the device in its sources and inputs).

llmConnectionHints.role: hardware interface node that reads from and writes to serial devices, storing received data in its own snapshot
llmConnectionHints.wiring: Observed-side: a DataPoint persists readings by listing this device in its sources (SOURCE_INVOKED) and inputs; to send commands, the device itself observes a trigger via its own sources.

llmExamples: Connect to the Zigbee coordinator on USB.
Set up a serial sensor on this server.
